content: Laravel ZIP upload security pitfalls: (1) Extracting files directly into web root (public_path() or base_path()) means uploaded PHP files become directly executable via HTTP — RCE. TenantSmith extracts to base_path('Modules/') which is within web root. (2) Zip Slip: malicious ZIPs with ../ path components write files outside target directory. TenantSmith's ModuleZipInspector checks for ../ and leading / but should also validate against symlink attacks. (3) Allowing dangerous file extensions inside ZIPs even for 'module' packages — migration files and seeders contain PHP that executes Artisan commands. (4) Not validating ZIP size before opening — denial of service via zip bomb. (5) Race condition between inspection and extraction — a different file could be swapped between inspect() and extract() calls if using temp file storage.
